name: Chicken Enchiladas in Green Mole
recipeIngredient: 4 Poblano peppers, roasted (peeled, seeded, and deveined)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine rice, chicken, cheese, sour cream, 1/2 cup chicken broth, onions, paprika, and salt in a large bowl. Mix thoroughly; set aside.
2. In a blender, combine remaining chicken broth, roasted peppers, almonds, cilantro, garlic, and black pepper. Process until smooth.
3. Heat oil in a heavy skillet over medium heat. Add the pepper mixture and cook over medium heat for 2 to 3 minutes, stirring constantly until thickened.
4. Reduce heat to low; add cream, remaining milk, and black pepper. Simmer uncovered for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally until slightly thickened. Set aside.
5. Dip each tortilla in the remaining 1 cup milk. Place a small skillet coated with cooking spray over low heat until hot.
6. Heat each tortilla until warm and soft. Remove tortillas and stack on a plate; keep warm.
7. Spoon 1/4 cup rice mixture in the center of each tortilla, folding sides over. Place in a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish coated with cooking spray.
8. Top with green mole sauce, cover, and bake at 350°F for 25 to 30 minutes, or until bubbly.
